name: Lisas Hard to Wreck It Fudge
recipeIngredient: 1 cup chopped pecans or walnuts, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 13X9-inch pan with foil. Butter the pan and set aside.
2. Butter the sides of a heavy 3-quart saucepan.
3. Combine the sugar, butter, and milk in the saucepan. Cook over medium-high heat until boiling, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon. Dissolving the sugar should take about 7 minutes.
4. Carefully clip a thermometer onto the inside of the pan.
5. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently until the thermometer reaches 236°F (soft ball stage). The mixture should be boiling at a moderate steady rate over the entire surface. Reaching soft ball stage should take 12-13 minutes at this boil rate.
6. Remove the saucepan from the heat and unclip the thermometer. Add chocolate, marshmallow cream, vanilla, and nuts. Stir quickly until well blended.
7. Turn out the mixture into the prepared pan. While the fudge is still warm, score the surface into individual pieces if desired.
8. Store in the fridge. Makes 3 1/2 pounds.
